extra.reasoning:
The property (monotonicity in `sweating`) holds for the entire input space. The decisive fact is that the input variable `sweating` does not appear in the condition of ANY rule. Enumerating all eight rules: raise_slurred_speech fires on slurred_speech, raise_dizziness on dizziness, raise_vomiting on vomiting, raise_back_pain on back_pain, raise_chest_pain on chest_pain, raise_syncope on syncope, raise_confusion on confusion, raise_stiff_neck on stiff_neck. None of them tests `sweating`. The Default (acuity := routine) applies only when no rule sets the output, and it also has no dependence on `sweating`.

Therefore, for any fixed assignment of the other eight inputs, the set of rules that fire is exactly the same whether `sweating` is false or true. Conflict resolution by priority (highest-priority matching rule wins, ties by listed order) and the default both operate solely on that firing set, so they produce an identical `acuity` value in both cases. That is, acuity(sweating=true) = acuity(sweating=false) for every assignment of the remaining inputs.

The property requires acuity(sweating=true) >= acuity(sweating=false) (no lower than before). Equality trivially satisfies a >= requirement, so the monotonicity property is never violated. No counterexample exists; both witness arrays are left empty.
